subject: Physics
overall_learning_objectives: Understand how day and night occur
Learn how the Sun helps measure time
Identify basic constellations in the sky
Recognize how early humans measured time using the sky

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Time and the Sky
topics_covered: Role of the Sun in measuring time
teaching_strategy: Use of globe and torch demonstration
student_activities: Observe how sunlight changes during the day
Explain why day and night occur

lesson_plan.title: Earth’s Rotation and Day-Night Cycle
topics_covered: How rotation causes day and night
student_activities: Rotate a globe to observe day and night

lesson_plan.title: Phases of the Moon
student_activities: Observe Moon shape for several days

lesson_plan.title: Constellations
topics_covered: Examples such as Orion and Ursa Major

lesson_plan.title: Eclipses
topics_covered: Alignment of Sun, Earth and Moon
teaching_strategy: Model demonstration using globe and ball
student_activities: Draw diagrams of solar and lunar eclipses

lesson_plan.title: Ancient Timekeeping and Revision
topics_covered: Importance of Sun and Moon in calendars
student_activities: Discuss how ancient people measured time

teacher_tip: Use visual demonstrations such as globe and torch models to help students clearly understand Earth’s rotation, Moon phases, and eclipse formation.
